title: Single-process vertical slices with one earned external seam
description: Place CLASSIFY inside Tally while preserving a deep public boundary and independently testable rule, evaluation, apply, and recovery behavior.
decision: Use typed vertical slices for Contract, Rules, Evaluation, Apply, Feedback, and Recovery in the existing Native-AOT executable. Keep domain policies pure, raw-SQLite and corpus infrastructure concrete, register dependencies explicitly, and reuse only the shared public Ledger operation executor seam. Do not add HTTP, a service, mediator, event bus, generic repository, plugin system, or speculative storage and filesystem ports.
rationale: The twelve explicit operation slices optimize for the actual caller and expose every invariant at the test surface. A three-facade design hides lifecycle and replay behind action discriminators. A port-heavy design invents seams with one production adapter. A separate service adds auth, deployment, and network failure. Embedding the behavior in Ledger reverses ownership. This adapts CORE explicit DI and vertical-slice guidance plus the established Ledger and Ingest single-process shape.
